title: John Berggruen Gives a Tour of Berggruen Gallery's 55 Year Anniversary Exhibition
content:
John Berggruen Gives a Tour of Berggruen Gallery's 55 Year Anniversary Exhibition
Berggruen Gallery
734 subscribers
14 likes
606 views
18 Jul 2025
In celebration of Berggruen Gallery’s 55th anniversary, we are pleased to present an exhibition walkthrough in which John Berggruen reflects on the gallery’s rich history and the many artists he has collaborated with over the years. 

55 Years: Isn't That Long Enough? is a celebratory group show marking over half a century of championing exceptional contemporary and modern art. Featuring paintings, works on paper, sculpture, film, and archival ephemera from the SFMOMA Library, and SFAI archive, this ambitious exhibition showcases museum-quality works by contemporary and historical artists, illustrating Berggruen Gallery's extensive curatorial history as well as its commitment to the development of the arts in the San Francisco Bay Area. Many of the works included in the show are on loan from private collections and have rarely been exhibited to the public. The exhibition will be on view from June 26 — August 14. The gallery will host an opening reception for the artists on Thursday, June 26, from 5–7 PM.

Berggruen Gallery—across its various spaces and iterations—has offered San Francisco a place where world-class art can be seen, contemplated, and collected for 55 years and counting. John Berggruen continues to energetically promote fine art through his deep curatorial knowledge, ever-present enthusiasm—and always with humor he might joke, “Fifty-five years—isn’t that long enough?”

Exhibiting Artists:  

Diana Al-Hadid | John Alexander | John Baldessari | David Bates | Robert Bechtle | Elmer Bischoff | Val Britton | Dean Byington | Alexander Calder | Bruce Cohen | Michael Craig-Martin | Stuart Davis | Heather Day | Roseline Delisle | Charles Demuth | Mark di Suvero | Richard Diebenkorn | Arthur Dove | Helen Frankenthaler | Isca Greenfield-Sanders | Michael Gregory | Peter Halley | Jane Hammond | Stephen Hannock | Mark Innerst | Ellsworth Kelly | Clare Kirkconnell | Matt Kleberg | Paul Kremer | Anna Kunz | Barry McGee | Tom McKinley | Julie Mehretu | Joan Miró | Claes Oldenburg | Nathan Oliveira | Tom Otterness | George Rickey | Ed Ruscha | Richard Serra | Joel Shapiro | Stephanie H. Shih | Frank Stella | Wayne Thiebaud | Darren Waterston | Lucy Williams | Donald Roller Wilson | Paul Wonner | Jonas Wood

title: Berggruen
content: ## Darren Waterston: A Tiny Radiance In a Dark Place. Publication Release, Jane Hammond: Chocolate Cosmos, String of Pearls. *Chocolate Cosmos, String of Pearls* presents the dazzling mixed-media botanical collages of New York artist Jane Hammond. Alongside these collages, made over the last decade, the book features a foreword by gallerist John Berggruen, an extended essay by the award-winning biographer and historian Jenny Uglow and a new text by Hammond herself. ## Darren Waterston Studio Visit.
